headline: Lucid Robotaxi Deals With Uber And Nuro Put Focus On Long-Term Story
summary: Lucid Group (NasdaqGS:LCID) is expanding its robotaxi partnerships with Uber and Nuro, with Uber increasing its deployment and investment in Lucid powered autonomous vehicles. The collaborations aim to integrate Lucid's vehicles and self driving technology into ride hailing and delivery services over time. These moves indicate a push beyond Lucid's core car sales into mobility services and technology partnerships. For investors following NasdaqGS:LCID, this partnership news arrives after a...

headline: Circle: Agentic's Uber moment hasn't arrived, yet
summary: Like most payment companies and banks, the stablecoin firm is lining up a suite of services for demand that hasn't quite materialized, but likely will – in some form.

headline: Watch CNBC's full interview with Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky
summary: Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky joins CNBC to discuss the new services, like grocery delivery and luggage storage, that will soon be available in the app. Chesky also shares what services he hopes to make available next, how they're capitalizing on the high demand from the World Cup, and much more.

headline: Jensen Huang Says Nvidia Is Powering Uber's Push Into Trillion-Dollar Self-Driving Opportunity Across 30 Cities
summary: Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang announced the chipmaker’s partnership with ride-hailing giant Uber Technologies Inc. (NYSE:UBER) will target global expansion of Robotaxis in the next two years. 30 Cities With Uber During NVIDIA’s earnings call with investors on Wednesday, Huang shared updates about the company’s self-driving exploits. “Our partnership with Uber will power the robotaxi fleet across nearly 30 cities and 4 continents by 2028,” he shared. headline: 1 S&P 500 Stock to Research Further and 2 We Brush Off
summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) is home to the biggest and most well-known companies in the market, making it a go-to index for investors seeking stability. But not all large-cap stocks are created equal - some are struggling with slowing growth, declining margins, or increased competition.
